clubs.name: Chess Club
clubs.description: The MVHS Chess Club is dedicated to helping members of the community with varying levels of skill and interest in chess to improve at the game while having fun playing. Chess Club includes teaching lessons to beginners, setting up online/local tournaments, and having in-person boards that members can use. Members can come to the chess club during lunch to play and talk with people who share a common interest in the game.

clubs.name: Dungeons and Dragons
clubs.description: The Dungeons & Dragons club aims to help students at MVHS explore and grow in the D&D community

clubs.name: Esports Club
clubs.description: The purpose of Mountain View Esports is to promote the growth of esports at Mountain View School by providing students with opportunities to engage in competitive gaming, develop skills in teamwork and strategy, and participate in local and national esports competitions. The club also aims to foster a positive gaming culture and build a supportive community among students

clubs.name: Crochet Club
clubs.description: The Crochet Club is a welcoming community where all those interested in crocheting can come and learn (including beginners)! We will provide free yarn, hooks, darning needles, and other related materials so all club members can learn at no expense to themselves. The Crochet Club will facilitate donation events to allow students who are interested in giving back to the community to do so (e.g. warm items such as beanies and scarves) and will also host semesterly volunteer events with local organizations to work with and bring a fun experience to residents. We will also teach specific fun projects to increase our club members’ interest in crocheting (e.g. flowers, bookmarks, and amigurumi).

clubs.name: Dance-a-Gram
clubs.description: Dance-a-Gram aims to spread joy and form a deeper connection with the senior community through coordinated dance performances. For seniors, watching dance can be a great way to relax, to bring back memories, and to spark conversation. The dance performances we conduct can brighten up their day and can even expose them to art forms and cultures that they’ve never interacted with before.

clubs.name: Drama Llamas
clubs.description: Drama Llamas is an entirely student-run club that puts on a full-scale musical at the end of each school year. The show is directed, teched, choreographed, managed, produced, performed, and sometimes even written by students of Mountain View High School. We were founded with the intent of making all aspects of theatre available to everyone, regardless of past experience. We are committed to creating student-run theater and learning leadership skills.

clubs.name: Asian Pacific Union
clubs.description: Asian Pacific Union (APU) aims to foster an environment that promotes the understanding of individual Asian American and Pacific Islander Heritage while still simultaneously creating Unity among students of all ethnic backgrounds. We also ensure that Asian Americans Pacific Islanders as a whole have a voice and community here at Mountain View High school. We appeal to everyone who identifies with Asian Pacific culture and those who want to learn more about Asian Pacific culture.

clubs.name: Barkada Club
clubs.meetingSchedule: Wednesday · First and last week of the month · Lunch
clubs.description: The Barkada Club aims to strengthen the Mountain View High School community by celebrating and sharing the Filipino culture while making new connections amongst other students. Our primary purpose is to serve as a bridge to bring together Filipino students and allies to share Filipino traditions, foods, and values that can help strengthen our heritage. The club serves as both a home away from home for Filipino students and as an educational platform for other students to learn about and appreciate the Filipino culture.

clubs.name: Black Student Union
clubs.description: The Black Student Union (BSU) is committed to fostering a safe, empowering, and inclusive space for Black students and allies to celebrate culture, build community, and promote academic and social excellence. Through advocacy, education, and engagement, BSU aims to uplift Black voices, honor heritage, and inspire unity and leadership within our school and beyond.

clubs.name: Aerospace & Aviation
clubs.description: The Aerospace & Aviation Club is a group whose members are interested in learning more about military and civil-grade aircraft, rockets, satellites, and more, and the physics and mathematics behind atmospheric and space flight.We will be doing lots of hands-on activities, such as building planes with various materials, water rockets, and detailed scale models. We will also fly RC planes with FPVs, and host building competitions and trivias within the club, where there will be rewards. We will have field trips to related institutes and bases, as well as guest speakers with experience in the aerospace and aviation industries. We serve to provide hands-on activities and practical knowledge to members of the MVHS community, as well as the opportunity to partake in hands-on activities they may not have individual access to.

clubs.name: Analytics Club
clubs.description: The Mountain View Analytics Club is a community of analytic enthusiasts passionate about exploring the world of data driven insights. Our club's primary purpose is to dive into the realm of analytics/statistics, particularly focusing on stock trading analytics and sports analytics. We also plan to engage competitively in the Wharton Global Youth Program, an international data science competition.

clubs.name: Artificial Intelligence Club
clubs.description: The Artificial Intelligence Club is for students curious about the real-world applications and impact of AI on our daily lives. Members will explore fundamental concepts, including linear regression and machine learning algorithms, alongside other crucial topics like AI ethics. We’ll offer hands-on experience with a variety of AI tools, from prompt engineering with large language models like ChatGPT to experiment with generative AI through Google’s AI experiments. Exciting guest speakers will come to share their cutting-edge perspectives, and our members will help organize events and workshops to help teach the broader community about Artificial Intelligence. Our goal is to empower members with a deep understanding of AI and its practical day-to-day use, fostering the next generation of AI-literate leaders.

clubs.name: Animal Welfare Club
clubs.description: The Animal Welfare Club hopes to spread awareness about animal-related issues and inspire students to participate in advocacy initiatives through activities, lectures, and projects. Students will have opportunities to research animal welfare problems and learn about how they can contribute to addressing these issues. We can provide mentorship to members who may be interested in designing and implementing their animal welfare projects. We will also organize service activities throughout the year open to everyone.

clubs.name: Cancer Awareness and Research Club
clubs.description: This organization shall have as its objective and purpose to increase knowledge and awareness about the various types of cancer and their profound impacts on our lives. Through a range of activities and workshops, we aim to educate students on important cancer-related topics. Our club will feature presentations and guest speakers who are experts in the field, providing valuable insights and information. Additionally, we will engage in collaborative group projects to research different aspects of cancer, fostering a deeper understanding among our members. To support the fight against cancer, we will organize fundraising events, with all proceeds going to cancer charities and societies.

clubs.name: CASC (California Association of Student Councils) Club
clubs.description: The CASC (California Association of Student Councils) Club aims to educate students around leadership and educational policy/legislation. Our club uses leadership curriculum to teach our students about how they can be leaders and how they can positively impact our community. In addition, our club focuses on educational legislation, creating proposals that often become policies for the California State Board of Education or into laws for the California legislative sector. Our parent organization, CASC, hosts different events supporting educational legislation on a local level and on a state level, many of which we will attend.

clubs.name: 6th Man
clubs.description: The 6th Man club is a school spirit club dedicated to building spirit and supporting Mountain View athletics. Our members being the student section at spiriting events, organize cheers, and create a fun and especially energetic atmosphere to encourage players and bring them

clubs.name: AAH (Animal Assisted Happiness)
clubs.description: AAH club connects the outside AAH organization with the special education classes and student body at MVHS by bringing our barnyard animals to campus for Spartan's Pause and vocational visits providing therapeutic services to our students. We further support the AAH farm by completing small projects such as painting birdhouses or making treats for barnyard animals!

clubs.name: Badminton Club
clubs.description: Badminton Clubs missions is to provide information for the upcoming season and connect newer players with eachother.
